message:
fix(init): require explicit consent for guard installation

Problem:
The init command previously auto-installed safety guards without user
consent in certain scenarios:

1. Claude Code hook (settings.json): Auto-installed silently when a
   .claude directory was detected, even in non-interactive mode
2. Git pre-commit hook: Auto-installed silently in non-interactive mode
   when a .git directory was detected
3. Repo init: Auto-installed git hook without any prompt

This violates the principle of least surprise and user consent. Users
may not expect or want:
- Modifications to their Claude Code settings
- Pre-commit hooks that could block their workflow
- Any system changes without explicit approval

Additionally, the isInteractive check was inconsistent:
- Some places used `options.interactive !== false` (opt-out)
- Other places used explicit TTY checks
- The logic was duplicated 4+ times

Solution:
1. Consolidate isInteractive check at function start:
   ```typescript
   const isInteractive =
     options.interactive === true &&  // Explicit opt-in (was !== false)
     !options.json &&
     process.stdin.isTTY &&
     process.stdout.isTTY;
   ```

2. Change from opt-out to opt-in for interactive mode:
   - Old: `options.interactive !== false` (interactive unless disabled)
   - New: `options.interactive === true` (interactive only if enabled)

3. Remove all auto-installation of guards:
   - Claude Code hook: Now prompts "Install Claude Code safety guard? [y/N]"
   - Git hook (global init): Now prompts "Install git pre-commit trauma guard? [y/N]"
   - Git hook (repo init): Removed auto-install entirely

4. Reuse isInteractive variable throughout:
   - Force confirmation prompt
   - Cross-agent enrichment prompt
   - Claude Code guard prompt (new)
   - Git hook prompt
   - Trauma scan

5. Improve skip messages to include CLI name dynamically:
   - "Skipped. You can install later with: cm guard --install"
   - Uses getCliName() for correct binary name

Files changed:
- src/commands/init.ts: initCommand() and initRepoCommand()

Behavioral changes:
- Guards are NEVER auto-installed anymore
- Guards are ONLY installed via interactive prompts with explicit consent
- Non-interactive/CI/programmatic usage will not install any guards
- Users can still manually install guards with `cm guard --install`

Security note:
This is a privacy/consent improvement. While the guards themselves are
beneficial security features, installing them without consent:
- Modifies user configuration unexpectedly
- Could interfere with existing workflows
- Violates principle of informed consent

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
